# @bug-on/md3-react — Full AI Documentation

This file contains the complete context for the `@bug-on/md3-react` library, including core principles, styling guides, and component references.

---

# PART 1: Core Principles & Architecture

# Core Principles & Architecture (AI Guide)

This guide helps AI agents understand how to write code for the `@bug-on/md3-react` library.

## 1. The Mental Model: MD3 Expressive
Unlike standard MD3, **Expressive** focus on:
- **Shape Morphing**: Components don't just change color; they change shape (e.g., a `Button` might go from a Pill to a Rounded Square when selected).
- **Spring Animations**: Use `SPRING_TRANSITION` (300ms equivalent) and `SPRING_TRANSITION_FAST` (200ms equivalent) for all transitions. Avoid `duration` based CSS transitions where possible.
- **Dynamic Color**: Colors are derived from CSS variables like `--m3-primary`, `--m3-surface-container-low`, etc.

## 2. Theme Management
The library uses a global provider. Always wrap your app in `MD3ThemeProvider`.

```tsx
import { MD3ThemeProvider } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

function App() {
  return (
    <MD3ThemeProvider mode="system" theme={customTheme}>
      <Main />
    </MD3ThemeProvider>
  );
}
```

## 3. Polymorphism (`asChild`)
Most components use the `asChild` pattern from Radix UI. This allows you to pass a custom element (like a Next.js `Link`) and have it receive the component's styles and logic.

```tsx
<Button asChild>
  <Link href="/dashboard">Go to Dashboard</Link>
</Button>
```
**AI Warning**: When using `asChild`, ensure the child is a single valid React element.

## 4. Animation & Motion
We use `motion/react` (Framer Motion).
- **Layout Animations**: Use `layout` or `layoutId` for smooth transitions between states.
- **Ripples**: Use `useRippleState` hook if creating custom interactive elements.

## 5. Tailwind Integration
We use Tailwind v4. The plugin automatically generates classes based on MD3 color roles:
- `bg-m3-primary`, `text-m3-on-primary`
- `bg-m3-surface-container-high`
- `border-m3-outline`

## 6. Icons
We use **Material Symbols** (Variable Font).
- Use the `<Icon name="..." />` component.
- The weight, grade, and optical size are handled automatically via CSS.

---

# PART 2: Styling & Design Tokens

# Styling & Design Tokens (AI Guide)

This guide explains how to style custom elements to match the `@bug-on/md3-react` system.

## 1. Tailwind v4 Plugin
The library provides a Tailwind plugin that maps MD3 color roles to utility classes.

### Color Roles
Use the `m3-` prefix followed by the MD3 role:
- **Primary**: `bg-m3-primary`, `text-m3-on-primary`, `border-m3-primary-container`
- **Surface**: `bg-m3-surface`, `bg-m3-surface-container-low`, `bg-m3-surface-variant`
- **Error**: `text-m3-error`, `bg-m3-error-container`

### State Opacity
MD3 states use opacity overlays. We provide shortcuts:
- `hover:bg-m3-primary/8` (8% opacity overlay)
- `active:bg-m3-primary/12` (12% opacity overlay)

## 2. Design Tokens
Tokens are available in `packages/tokens`.

### Typography Scale
Use these Tailwind classes for standard MD3 type scales:
- `text-m3-display-large`, `text-m3-headline-medium`, `text-m3-title-small`
- `text-m3-body-large`, `text-m3-label-medium`

### Shapes
Shapes in MD3 Expressive are dynamic.
- `rounded-m3-none`: 0px
- `rounded-m3-extra-small`: 4px
- `rounded-m3-small`: 8px
- `rounded-m3-medium`: 12px
- `rounded-m3-large`: 16px
- `rounded-m3-extra-large`: 28px
- `rounded-m3-full`: Pill (9999px or half-height)

## 3. Motion (Springs)
We prefer springs over durations. When using Framer Motion, use these constants:

```tsx
import { SPRING_TRANSITION, SPRING_TRANSITION_FAST } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<m.div animate={{ scale: 1 }} transition={SPRING_TRANSITION} />
```

- `SPRING_TRANSITION`: Standard damping, good for general movement.
- `SPRING_TRANSITION_FAST`: Higher stiffness, good for subtle feedback (hover/tap).
- `SPRING_TRANSITION_EXPRESSIVE`: High bounce (0.45) for active indicators and "pop" effects.

## 4. Icon Styles
Icons should always use the `Icon` component for consistent variable font settings:
```tsx
<Icon name="search" weight={500} size={24} />
```
Available weights: 100, 200, 300, 400 (default), 500, 600, 700.

---

# PART 3: Component Reference (Standardized MD3 Expressive)

> **AI Note**: All components below have been standardized to follow Material Design 3 Expressive guidelines. Use these APIs for high-fidelity implementations. All components support `asChild` (polymorphism) unless otherwise noted.

---

## Setup Requirements

```tsx
// 1. Import CSS (REQUIRED — icons will not render without this)
import "@bug-on/md3-react/index.css";
import "@bug-on/md3-react/material-symbols.css"; // if using <Icon>

// 2. Wrap the app in providers
import { MD3ThemeProvider, SnackbarProvider } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

function Root() {
  return (
    <MD3ThemeProvider mode="system">
      <SnackbarProvider>
        <App />
      </SnackbarProvider>
    </MD3ThemeProvider>
  );
}
```

---

## 1. Icon

Material Symbols variable font component. Standardized for high performance and accessibility. Always `aria-hidden="true"`.

```tsx
import { Icon } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

// Basic
<Icon name="home" />

// Full API Reference
<Icon
  name="favorite"          // string — REQUIRED. Ligature name in snake_case (e.g., "arrow_forward")
  variant="outlined"       // "outlined" | "rounded" | "sharp" (default: "outlined")
  fill={1}                 // 0 | 1 — 0 for outlined, 1 for solid (default: 0)
  weight={400}             // 100, 200, 300, 400, 500, 600, 700 (default: 400)
  grade={0}                // -50 | -25 | 0 | 100 | 200 — Optical weight adjustment
  opticalSize={24}         // 20 | 24 | 40 | 48 — Geometric optimization (default: 24)
  size={24}                // number | "inherit" — Visual font-size in pixels
  animateFill              // boolean — Enables spring-based morphing for the FILL axis
/>
```

---

## 2. Button

High-fidelity action component with expressive motion and polymorphism support.

```tsx
import { Button } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

// Standard Button
<Button
  colorStyle="filled"      // "elevated" | "filled" | "tonal" | "outlined" | "text" (default: "filled")
  size="sm"                // "xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" (default: "sm")
  shape="round"            // "round" (pill) | "square" (rounded-rect) (default: "round")
  icon={<Icon name="add" />}      // Leading/trailing graphic
  iconPosition="leading"   // "leading" | "trailing" (default: "leading")
  loading={false}          // Replaces content with a loading indicator
  loadingVariant="loading-indicator" // "loading-indicator" (morphing) | "circular"
  disabled={false}
  asChild={false}          // Merge props onto child element (Polymorphism)
>
  Label
</Button>

// Toggle Button — REQUIRES variant="toggle" AND selected prop
<Button variant="toggle" selected={isActive} onClick={toggle}>
  Filter
</Button>

// Link Button via asChild
<Button asChild>
  <a href="/docs">View Docs</a>
</Button>
```

---

## 3. IconButton

Icon-only action component. `aria-label` is **required**.

```tsx
import { IconButton } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<IconButton
  aria-label="Close"           // string — REQUIRED for accessibility
  colorStyle="standard"        // "standard" | "filled" | "tonal" | "outlined" (default: "standard")
  size="md"                    // "xs" | "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" (default: "md")
  shape="round"                // "round" | "square" (default: "round")
  loading={false}
  onClick={handleClose}
>
  <Icon name="close" />
</IconButton>

// Toggle Variant — Requires variant="toggle" and selected
<IconButton
  variant="toggle"
  selected={isLiked}
  aria-label="Like"
  onClick={() => setLiked(!liked)}
>
  <Icon name={isLiked ? "favorite" : "favorite_border"} />
</IconButton>
```

---

## 4. FAB (Floating Action Button)

High-emphasis action button with extended label support and shape-morphing.

```tsx
import { FAB, FABPosition } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<FAB
  icon={<Icon name="add" />}    // ReactNode — REQUIRED
  size="md"                     // "sm" | "md" | "lg" | "xl" (default: "md")
  colorStyle="primary"          // "primary" | "secondary" | "tertiary" | "surface"
  extended={false}              // boolean — Reveals label with fluid width animation
  lowered={false}               // boolean — Reduces shadow elevation for sub-menus
  visible={true}                // boolean — Controls entrance/exit scale animation
  aria-label="Create item"      // REQUIRED if label is not provided via children
>
  Create                        {/* Label content for extended FAB */}
</FAB>

// Fixed Positioning Helper
<FABPosition position="bottom-right"> // "bottom-right" | "bottom-left" | "top-right" | "top-left"
  <FAB icon={<Icon name="send" />} />
</FABPosition>
```

---

## 5. Badge & BadgedBox

Compact status indicators for notifications and counts.

```tsx
import { Badge, BadgedBox } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

// Dot Badge (Decorative signal)
<Badge />

// Numeric Badge (Counts)
<Badge max={99}>{count}</Badge>   // Automatically appends "+" if count > max

// BadgedBox — Positioning orchestrator
<BadgedBox badge={<Badge>{count}</Badge>}>
  <IconButton icon={<Icon name="notifications" />} />
</BadgedBox>

// Explicit sizing for manual offsets
<BadgedBox badge={<Badge />} badgeSize="small">
  <Icon name="mail" />
</BadgedBox>
```

---

## 6. Card

Surface component for grouping content. Supports elevation, filling, and outlining.

```tsx
import { Card } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

// Static Card
<Card variant="elevated">
  <div className="p-4">Content</div>
</Card>

// Interactive Card (as Button)
<Card variant="filled" onClick={() => handleAction()}>
  <div className="p-4">Clickable content</div>
</Card>

// Polymorphic Link Card
<Card variant="outlined" asChild>
  <a href="/details">View Details</a>
</Card>

// API Reference
// variant: "elevated" | "filled" | "outlined" (default: "elevated")
// disabled: boolean — Lowers opacity and prevents clicks
// className: string — Custom styling
```

---

## 7. Chip

Compact interactive elements for actions, filtering, input, or suggestions.

```tsx
import { Chip } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

// Assist Chip (Actions)
<Chip variant="assist" label="Add to plan" icon={<Icon name="add" />} />

// Filter Chip (Selection)
<Chip 
  variant="filter" 
  label="Favorites" 
  selected={isFav} 
  onClick={() => setFav(!isFav)} 
/>

// Input Chip (Entities/Tags)
<Chip 
  variant="input" 
  label="John Doe" 
  onRemove={() => removeUser()} 
  avatar={<img src="/user.png" />} 
/>

// Suggestion Chip (Hints)
<Chip variant="suggestion" label="Try this" elevated />

// API Reference
// label: ReactNode — REQUIRED
// selected: boolean — For filter/input variants
// elevated: boolean — Adds shadow depth
// leadingIcon: ReactNode
// avatar: ReactNode — Circular graphic (takes priority over leadingIcon)
// onRemove: () => void — Enables the close (✕) button
```

---

## 8. Checkbox

Selection control for multiple choices or tri-state logic.

```tsx
import { Checkbox, TriStateCheckbox } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

// Basic 2-State
<Checkbox
  label="Accept terms"
  checked={checked}
  onCheckedChange={setChecked}
/>

// Error State
<Checkbox label="Required" error checked={false} />

// Tri-State (Mixed)
<TriStateCheckbox
  label="Select All"
  state={parentState} // "checked" | "unchecked" | "indeterminate"
  onStateChange={setParentState}
/>

// Forced Indeterminate
<Checkbox label="Child options" indeterminate />
```

---

## 9. Switch

Binary toggle for immediate settings.

```tsx
import { Switch } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<Switch
  label="Dark Mode"
  checked={isDark}
  onCheckedChange={setDark}
  icons // Enables icons inside the thumb
  thumbContent={isDark ? <Icon name="dark_mode" /> : <Icon name="light_mode" />}
/>

// API Reference
// checked: boolean — REQUIRED
// onCheckedChange: (val: boolean) => void — REQUIRED
// icons: boolean — Display thumb content in both states
// showOnlySelectedIcon: boolean — Only show thumb content when ON
```

---

## 10. Radio Button

Selection control for mutually exclusive choices.

```tsx
import { RadioGroup, RadioButton } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<RadioGroup 
  name="plan" 
  defaultValue="free" 
  onValueChange={(val) => console.log(val)}
>
  <RadioButton value="free" label="Free Plan" />
  <RadioButton value="pro" label="Pro Plan" />
  <RadioButton value="enterprise" label="Enterprise" disabled />
</RadioGroup>

// API Reference
// orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical" (default: "vertical")
// error: boolean (on RadioButton) — Visual error state
```

---

## 11. Text Field

Comprehensive input component with floating labels and expressive transitions.

```tsx
import { TextField } from "@bug-on/md3-react";
import type { TextFieldHandle } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<TextField
  label="Username"
  variant="outlined"        // "filled" | "outlined" (default: "filled")
  type="text"               // Support all native types + "textarea"
  value={val}
  onChange={(v) => setVal(v)} // NOTE: First arg is string value
  supportingText="Required"
  error={!!err}
  errorText={err}
  leadingIcon={<Icon name="person" />}
  trailingIconMode="clear"  // "none" | "clear" | "password-toggle" | "custom"
  dense                     // 48px height instead of 56px
  autoResize                // For "textarea" type
/>

// Imperative API
const ref = useRef<TextFieldHandle>(null);
<TextField ref={ref} ... />
ref.current?.focus();
ref.current?.clear();
```

> **AI Gotcha**: `onChange` receives `(value: string, event)` NOT `(event)`. The first argument is the new value string.

---

## 12. Tabs

Categorical navigation within a view. Built on Radix UI.

```tsx
import { Tabs, TabsList, Tab, TabsContent } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<Tabs defaultValue="overview">
  <TabsList variant="primary"> {/* "primary" | "secondary" — REQUIRED */}
    <Tab value="overview">Overview</Tab>
    <Tab value="details" icon={<Icon name="info" />}>Details</Tab>
    <Tab value="settings" badge={<Badge />}>Settings</Tab>
  </TabsList>
  <TabsContent value="overview">Overview content</TabsContent>
</Tabs>

// API Reference
// variant: "primary" (short indicator) | "secondary" (full-width)
// scrollable: boolean — Enables horizontal scrolling
// inlineIcon: boolean (on Tab) — Positions icon beside label
```

---

## 13. Navigation Rail

Vertical navigation for medium-to-large screens.

```tsx
import { NavigationRail, NavigationRailItem } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<NavigationRail
  variant="collapsed" // "collapsed" | "expanded" | "modal" | "xr"
  fab={<FAB icon={<Icon name="add" />} />}
>
  <NavigationRailItem
    label="Home"
    icon={<Icon name="home" />}
    selected={active === "home"}
    onClick={() => setActive("home")}
  />
</NavigationRail>

// API Reference
// variant: "collapsed" (rail) | "expanded" (drawer) | "modal" | "xr" (floating)
// labelVisibility: "labeled" | "auto" | "unlabeled"
// activeIndicatorTransition: Transition — Custom spring config for the active pill
```

> **AI Gotcha**: When using `<Icon>` for the `icon` prop, `NavigationRail` automatically adds `fill={selected ? 1 : 0}` and `animateFill` — no need to set these manually.

---

## 14. Navigation Bar

Horizontal navigation for compact screens (mobile/tablet).

```tsx
import { NavigationBar, NavigationBarItem, Icon } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<NavigationBar
  variant="flexible" // "flexible" | "baseline" | "xr"
  hideOnScroll={true}
>
  <NavigationBarItem
    label="Home"
    icon={<Icon name="home" />}
    selected={active === "home"}
    onClick={() => setActive("home")}
  />
</NavigationBar>

// API Reference
// variant: "flexible" (responsive) | "baseline" (fixed height) | "xr" (orbiter)
// itemLayout: "vertical" | "horizontal" (overrides responsive)
// hideOnScroll: boolean
// elevated: boolean
// activeIndicatorTransition: Transition — Custom spring config for the active pill
```

---

## 15. App Bar

Top and bottom screen headers/footers with dynamic scroll behaviors.

```tsx
import { 
  SmallAppBar, 
  MediumFlexibleAppBar, 
  LargeFlexibleAppBar, 
  BottomAppBar 
} from "@bug-on/md3-react";

// Standard Small App Bar
<SmallAppBar
  title="Page Title"
  navigationIcon={<IconButton icon={<Icon name="menu" />} />}
  actions={<IconButton icon={<Icon name="search" />} />}
/>

// Bottom App Bar with FAB
<BottomAppBar
  actions={<IconButton icon={<Icon name="menu" />} />}
  fab={<FAB icon={<Icon name="add" />} size="md" lowered />}
/>

// API Reference
// scrollBehavior: Result of useAppBarScroll hook
// behavior: "pinned" | "enterAlways" | "exitUntilCollapsed"
```

---

## 16. Dialog

Modal overlays for critical information and decisions.

```tsx
import { 
  Dialog, DialogTrigger, DialogContent, 
  DialogTitle, DialogBody, DialogFooter 
} from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<Dialog>
  <DialogTrigger asChild><Button>Open</Button></DialogTrigger>
  <DialogContent>
    <DialogTitle>Confirm Action</DialogTitle>
    <DialogBody>Are you sure you want to proceed?</DialogBody>
    <DialogFooter>
      <DialogClose asChild><Button colorStyle="text">Cancel</Button></DialogClose>
      <Button onClick={handleConfirm}>Confirm</Button>
    </DialogFooter>
  </DialogContent>
</Dialog>

// Full-screen Variant
<DialogFullScreenContent 
  title="New Event" 
  actionLabel="Save" 
  onAction={handleSave}
>
  Content...
</DialogFullScreenContent>
```

> **AI Gotcha**: `DialogBody` uses `ScrollArea` internally and has a max-height of `calc(85dvh - 200px)`. For very short dialogs you may want to pass `className="max-h-none"`.

---

## 17. Drawer (Bottom Sheet)

Responsive container that slides up from the bottom of the screen.

```tsx
import { 
  Drawer, DrawerTrigger, DrawerContent, 
  DrawerTitle, DrawerHeader, DrawerFooter 
} from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<Drawer>
  <DrawerTrigger asChild><Button>Open Sheet</Button></DrawerTrigger>
  <DrawerContent>
    <DrawerHeader>
      <DrawerTitle>Select Option</DrawerTitle>
    </DrawerHeader>
    <div className="p-4">Content goes here...</div>
    <DrawerFooter>
      <DrawerClose asChild><Button>Close</Button></DrawerClose>
    </DrawerFooter>
  </DrawerContent>
</Drawer>

// API Reference
// maxHeight: number — Limit sheet height
// hideHandle: boolean — Hide the drag handle
```

---

## 18. Snackbar

Brief messages about app processes. Features a queuing system for sequential display.

```tsx
import { SnackbarProvider, useSnackbar } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

// 1. Wrap your app
<SnackbarProvider><App /></SnackbarProvider>

// 2. Trigger from any child
const { showSnackbar } = useSnackbar();

const trigger = async () => {
  const result = await showSnackbar({
    message: "Message deleted",
    actionLabel: "Undo",
    duration: "short", // "short" (4s) | "long" (10s)
  });
  
  if (result === "action-performed") handleUndo();
};
```

---

## 19. Tooltip

Informative text that appears on focus or hover.

```tsx
import { PlainTooltip, RichTooltip } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

// Plain Tooltip (Simple label)
<PlainTooltip content="Edit profile">
  <IconButton icon={<Icon name="edit" />} />
</PlainTooltip>

// Rich Tooltip (Header + Description + Optional Actions)
<RichTooltip 
  title="Permissions" 
  description="Grant access to camera and microphone."
>
  <IconButton icon={<Icon name="security" />} />
</RichTooltip>
```

---

## 20. Slider

Control for selecting values from a range. Supports discrete and continuous modes.

```tsx
import { Slider, RangeSlider } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

// Single Handle
<Slider defaultValue={[50]} min={0} max={100} step={1} />

// Range (Dual Handles)
<RangeSlider defaultValue={[20, 80]} />

// API Reference
// variant: "continuous" | "discrete"
// showTickMarks: boolean — For discrete sliders
```

---

## 21. Scroll Area

Custom scrollbar implementation with MD3 styling and fluid visibility.

```tsx
import { ScrollArea } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<ScrollArea type="hover" className="h-64">
  <div className="p-4">Long content...</div>
</ScrollArea>

// API Reference
// type: "auto" | "always" | "scroll" | "hover"
// orientation: "vertical" | "horizontal" | "both"
```

---

## 22. Menu

Contextual list of actions or options. Supports submenus and checkboxes.

```tsx
import { Menu, MenuTrigger, MenuContent, MenuItem } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<Menu>
  <MenuTrigger asChild><Button>Options</Button></MenuTrigger>
  <MenuContent>
    <MenuItem icon={<Icon name="edit" />}>Edit</MenuItem>
    <MenuItem icon={<Icon name="share" />}>Share</MenuItem>
    <MenuDivider />
    <MenuItem variant="danger" icon={<Icon name="delete" />}>Delete</MenuItem>
  </MenuContent>
</Menu>

// Also available: SubMenu, ContextMenu, MenuCheckboxItem
```

---

## 23. Divider

Visual rule for separating content. Supports expressive wavy shapes.

```tsx
import { Divider } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

// Standard
<Divider />

// Expressive Wavy
<Divider shape="wavy" />

// Inset for lists
<Divider variant="inset" insetStart="icon" />

// API Reference
// variant: "full-bleed" | "inset" | "middle-inset" | "subheader"
// shape: "flat" | "wavy"
// orientation: "horizontal" | "vertical"
```

---

## 24. Button Group

Groups related buttons with shared borders and expressive shape-morphing.

```tsx
import { ButtonGroup, Button } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<ButtonGroup>
  <Button colorStyle="outlined">Left</Button>
  <Button colorStyle="outlined">Center</Button>
  <Button colorStyle="outlined">Right</Button>
</ButtonGroup>

// API Reference
// connected: boolean — If true, removes margins between buttons (Segmented style)
// fullWidth: boolean
```

---

## 25. Progress & Loading Indicators

Visual signals for status and progress.

```tsx
import { ProgressIndicator, LoadingIndicator } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

// Linear Progress
<ProgressIndicator variant="linear" value={40} />

// Circular Progress
<ProgressIndicator variant="circular" value={40} />

// Morphing Loading Indicator (Standard MD3 Expressive)
<LoadingIndicator size="md" />

// API Reference
// value: number (0-100) — If omitted, shows indeterminate animation
```

---

## 26. Theme Provider & Hooks

```tsx
import { MD3ThemeProvider, useTheme, useThemeMode } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

// Provider
<MD3ThemeProvider
  mode="system"             // "light" | "dark" | "system" (default: "system")
  theme={customColorScheme} // optional — generated from generateM3Theme()
>
  <App />
</MD3ThemeProvider>

// Hooks (inside provider)
const { mode, setMode, effectiveMode } = useThemeMode();
// effectiveMode: "light" | "dark" — resolved system preference

const { theme } = useTheme();
```

---

## 27. Typography

Expressive type system powered by Google Sans Flex.

```tsx
import { Text } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<Text variant="displayLarge">Welcome</Text>
<Text variant="bodyMedium" as="p">This is a paragraph.</Text>

// Variants: 
//   displayLarge, displayMedium, displaySmall
//   headlineLarge, headlineMedium, headlineSmall
//   titleLarge, titleMedium, titleSmall
//   bodyLarge, bodyMedium, bodySmall
//   labelLarge, labelMedium, labelSmall
//   (Also supported: emphasized variants like displayLargeEmphasized)
```

---

## 28. Ripple (Low-level)

For custom interactive components.

```tsx
import { Ripple, useRippleState } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

function CustomButton({ children, onClick }) {
  const { ripples, onPointerDown, removeRipple } = useRippleState({
    disabled: false,
  });

  return (
    <button
      className="relative overflow-hidden"
      onPointerDown={onPointerDown}
      onClick={onClick}
    >
      <Ripple ripples={ripples} onRippleDone={removeRipple} />
      {children}
    </button>
  );
}
```

---

## 29. Text (Raw)

Simple span with MD3 typography styles.

```tsx
import { Text } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<Text as="p" variant="body-medium" className="text-m3-on-surface">
  Paragraph content
</Text>
```

---

## 30. CodeBlock

Syntax-highlighted code display.

```tsx
import { CodeBlock } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<CodeBlock language="tsx">
  {`const x = 1;`}
</CodeBlock>
```

---

## 31. TableOfContents

Auto-generated TOC from headings.

```tsx
import { TableOfContents } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<TableOfContents
  items={[
    { id: "section-1", label: "Introduction", level: 1 },
    { id: "section-2", label: "Usage", level: 2 },
  ]}
  activeId={activeSection}
/>
```

---

## 32. Search

Comprehensive search interface with docking and fullscreen support.

```tsx
import { Search } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<Search
  placeholder="Search files..."
  value={query}
  onChange={setQuery}
  variant="docked" // "docked" | "fullscreen"
  leadingIcon={<Icon name="search" />}
/>
```

---

## 33. FAB Menu (Speed Dial)

Staggered menu that expands from a FAB.

```tsx
import { FABMenu } from "@bug-on/md3-react";

<FABMenu
  expanded={isOpen}
  onToggle={setIsOpen}
  items={[
    { id: "1", label: "Image", icon: <Icon name="image" />, onClick: () => {} },
    { id: "2", label: "Video", icon: <Icon name="videocam" />, onClick: () => {} },
  ]}
/>
```

---

## 34. Quick Common Mistakes

| Mistake | Correct |
|---------|---------|
| `<Icon name="ArrowForward" />` | `<Icon name="arrow_forward" />` |
| `<Button variant="toggle">` (no `selected`) | Must also pass `selected={boolean}` |
| `<IconButton>` (no `aria-label`) | `aria-label` is required for accessibility |
| `onChange={e => setValue(v)}` | `onChange={(value) => setValue(value)}` (TextField uses string as 1st arg) |
| `<TabsList>` (no `variant`) | `variant="primary" \| "secondary"` is required |
| `useSnackbar()` outside Provider | Ensure `<SnackbarProvider>` wraps the component tree |
